author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Matrix axes

Scale; graph topology; edge-kind family; history age; mutation concurrency; index state; query budget; adapter; serialization; schema version; failure injection; permissions or redaction; exact versus approximate algorithm.

## Required adversarial cases

Dense cliques; long chains; wide stars; duplicate aliases; associative cycles; genuine ordering cycles; dangling imported endpoints; self-links; contradictory inverses; high-degree hubs; temporal supersession; checkpoint corruption; concurrent retyping; stale cursors; severe token limits.

## Reporting

Default CI output is a compact pass or fail envelope with deltas. Full evidence is stored as artifacts or linked results and remains available by case, seed, snapshot, and scale. No benchmark may silently skip or downscale.
Graph conformance and scale verification matrix: property, replay, migration, concurrency, token-budget, and million-item tests
Build a reusable verification program for relationship schemas, immutable mutations, bounded queries, analytics, governance, adapters, and reconstruction waves.

Fixtures cover empty and seconds-old scratch projects, representative medium histories, deep and cyclic typed graphs, multi-decade event streams, disconnected archives, and deterministic 10k, 100k, and one-million-item workspaces; Property tests cover registry invariants, inverse and alias rules, append-only mutation, replay equivalence, snapshot isolation, cursor stability, migration, merge or federation, and correction by superseding event; Algorithm tests verify exact results on bounded fixtures and deterministic seeds, error bounds, freshness, provenance, and monotonic resource limits for approximations; Concurrency tests exercise thousands-agent read and write contention, crashes, stale indexes, retries, idempotency, checkpoints, and non-overlapping reconstruction claims; CLI, MCP, SDK, extension, and shell pipeline parity share golden semantics while allowing intentionally different compact projections; Performance gates record p50, p95, and p99 latency, peak memory, incremental update cost, bytes and tokens, truncation quality, and index rebuild time with documented hardware and envelopes; Every test runs in sandboxed PM_PATH and PM_GLOBAL_PATH and never reads or writes the real repository tracker
Trustworthy typed graph context from tiny to million-item workspaces
Prove every graph capability and adapter against one adversarial, replayable, scale-aware conformance matrix
Implementers get a stable completion gate and extensions can verify compatibility without duplicating fixtures
Catches semantic drift, nondeterminism, hidden full scans, replay failures, false cycles, concurrency races, and token regressions before release
Public test fixtures and helpers, property suites, golden projections, scale envelopes, adapter parity checks, and reconstruction-wave gates
The graph program spans SDK, CLI, MCP, packages, history, indexes, analytics, and audits, so acceptance criteria need one explicit cross-cutting verification owner

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: pm-mi2x supplies generic scale fixtures and pm-atfm evaluates context quality,but no item owns typed-graph property,replay,migration,adapter-parity,and reconstruction conformance across those fixtures.

message: Create graph conformance and scale verification chore
